design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate levels, rescue neighbors from monsters, collect varied weapons, defeat enemies, reach exit door
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Neighbors are continuously threatened and killed by enemies; player must balance offense/rescue against time pressure and resource conservation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Teenage underdogs armed with absurd unconventional weaponry fighting B-movie horror monsters to save their suburban community
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deeply committed to horror film pastiche with weapon-monster matching (silverware vs werewolves, crucifixes vs vampires); quirky humor and off-beat tone; brutal level design that rewards pattern learning and resource management over raw reflexes

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives; losing all lives ends game; earning points grants extra lives
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-level checkpoints; full level restart on death
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Rescue of at least one neighbor per level required to progress; daytime-to-nightfall mechanic forces adaptive strategy; weapon selection against specific enemy types provides strategic depth

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickup system with varied effectiveness against enemy types; health packs restore vitality; potions provide temporary buffs (speed increase, monster transformation); keys open doors
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven optional bonus levels; progression blocked if all neighbors killed or all lives lost
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon effectiveness encourages different tactical approaches
Score-based ranking system (neighbors rescued worth different points)
Daytime-to-nightfall mechanic creates variable level conditions
48 total stages with optional bonus content

world_building_techniques: Level theming tied to specific monster archetypes (suburban neighborhoods, shopping malls, pyramids, haunted castles)
Neighbor character variety (barbecue chefs, teachers, babies, tourists, archeologists, soldiers, cheerleaders) reinforces suburban slice-of-life context
Monster roster based on classic 1950s horror films and modern slasher movies
Time-of-day transitions (daytime to nightfall with mechanical consequences like werewolf transformation)
End goal: confront Dr. Tongue in his castle to end monster attacks

boss_design.philosophy: Final confrontation against Dr. Tongue (mad scientist antagonist); inferred to be thematically tied to master of monster creation
notable_bosses: Dr. Tongue (final boss; creator of all monsters)

faction_indicators: Two-player cooperative mode suggests shared purpose between Zeke and Julie
Neighbors as distinct archetypal NPCs (tourists, soldiers, teachers) represent diverse community stakeholders
danger_signals: Nightfall mechanic transforms innocent tourists into hostile werewolves—environmental threat escalation
Dr. Tongue's unleashed monsters terrorizing suburban areas—active, expanding threat
Neighbors continuously killed by enemies—civilian casualty pressure on player
Castle stronghold of Dr. Tongue—centralized source of danger
environmental_storytelling: Suburban neighborhood setting contrasts mundane American life against horror invasion
Shopping mall, pyramid, and castle progression traces Dr. Tongue's influence across varied territories
Enemies and weapons tied to specific film tropes create implicit lore (e.g., crucifix effectiveness vs vampires references Christian symbolism in vampire folklore)
